question.text: What is the significance of Section 15Y of the SEBI Act regarding civil court jurisdiction?

answer.text: No civil court can entertain suits or proceedings on matters within SAT's jurisdiction, and no injunction can be granted against SEBI actions
Civil courts can hear appeals against SEBI orders in parallel with SAT
Civil courts can grant temporary injunctions against SEBI actions
Civil courts have superior jurisdiction over SAT

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15Y bars civil courts from entertaining suits or proceedings on matters within SAT's jurisdiction. Further, no injunction can be granted by any court or authority in respect of any action taken or to be taken under the SEBI Act. **The Trap:** There is NO parallel jurisdiction or temporary injunction power. SAT has exclusive jurisdiction for SEBI Act matters. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This exclusion ensures regulatory actions cannot be stalled by civil court proceedings — only SAT and then the Supreme Court can review SEBI decisions.
